markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Show when Loom tags/backlinks/pins influence retrieval and expose graph-bias reasons. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: LoomRetrievalBiasVisibility leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: LoomRetrievalBiasVisibility is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: Loom graph-search block hits now rank by bounded Loom-native retrieval-bias score from pinned/favorite/tag_count/backlink_count, expose hsk.loom_retrieval_bias@1 metadata with reason codes and raw-vs-scoring counts, and propagate that metadata into the Loom visual-debug search summary. UserManual registry/seed documents the graph-search metadata contract and bumps USER_MANUAL_VERSION.
lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: MT-189 focused real-PostgreSQL proof passes, adjacent graph-search and visual-debug regressions pass, UserManual content/storage tests pass, and adversarial review found no blocking findings. Low count-ambiguity review finding was remediated by adding raw count plus score_count/score_count_cap metadata.

adversarial_review.summary: No blocking MT-189 findings. Reviewer confirmed ranking formula, graph-search metadata path, API serialization boundary, visual-debug metadata consumer, and UserManual discoverability. Low residual count ambiguity was remediated after review.
residual_risks: search_loom_graph concatenates Loom block hits before knowledge_entities/UserManual hits, then paginates the combined vector. This predates MT-189, but Loom retrieval-bias scoring can make the Loom block section more prominent. Treat graph-search as discovery/projection unless a later MT introduces cross-source source-priority semantics.

Product files touched for MT-189: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs, src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/loom.rs, src/backend/handshake_core/tests/loom_retrieval_bias_visibility_tests.rs, src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/mod.rs, src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/registry.rs, src/backend/handshake_core/src/user_manual/seed.rs, src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml.
The MT-189 fixture uses real/managed PostgreSQL through knowledge_pg_support; there is no SQLite, Docker-default, external daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof path.

Graph-search metadata exposes retrieval_bias_schema_id, retrieval_bias_score, and retrieval_bias_reasons for Loom block hits; plain hits expose an empty reason list; visual-debug search summaries preserve the same metadata for model/debug inspection.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
